---
title: "DuckDB-WASM Phase 0 — step 4 (WebR + DuckDB-WASM coexistence, native shinylive)"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: false
    resources:
      - shinylive-sw.js
      - test.parquet
filters:
  - shinylive
---

## Goal

Verify that DuckDB-WASM (JS module, Web Worker) and Shinylive/WebR can coexist in the
same browser tab without OOM or Worker conflicts. Part of llmtelemetry issue
[#83](https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/issues/83) Phase 0 feasibility.

**Render approach:** This step uses the native `{shinylive-r}` Quarto chunk. The `shinylive`
R package was added to the project nix shell on 2026-05-16, so the filter renders at build
time and bundles WebR inline.

**Architecture note (corrected):** The `{shinylive-r}` chunk always renders the Shiny app
inside a Shinylive-managed `<iframe>`. This is how the Shinylive runtime works — regardless
of YAML options, the R/Shiny app runs in a separate browsing context (the iframe) with its
own origin and JS heap. DuckDB-WASM runs in the **parent page's** JS context. They are in
**different browsing contexts**, not the same one.

This prototype therefore tests *coexistence* (both runtimes load in the same browser tab
without OOM or Worker conflicts) rather than *same-context integration*. Cross-context
communication between the DuckDB-WASM parent and the Shinylive iframe requires the
`postMessage` API (cross-origin messaging). See issue #83 for the follow-on design.

## What to check in DevTools

- **Memory**: total tab usage after both panels fully load. Expected combined: ~500 MB.
  Alarm if above 2 GB.
- **Console**: no `SharedArrayBuffer` conflicts, no Worker registration errors from either
  DuckDB-WASM or WebR/Shinylive.
- **Network**: DuckDB-WASM fetches from `cdn.jsdelivr.net`; the Shinylive `<iframe>`
  fetches from `shinylive.io`. Both should load independently without blocking each other.
- **Performance**: note when the Shinylive iframe shows "WebR alive" — this is WebR cold
  load time. The iframe is a separate browsing context; its load does not block DuckDB-WASM.
- **Context separation**: DuckDB runs in the parent page; WebR/Shiny runs in the Shinylive
  iframe. They share the same browser tab but NOT the same JS heap or origin.

## DuckDB-WASM block (identical to step 3)

<pre id="output" style="background:#1a1a1a; color:#0f0; padding:1em; min-height:200px; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: monospace;">Loading DuckDB-WASM…</pre>

<script type="module">
  const out = document.getElementById('output');
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const ms = () => `[${Math.round(performance.now() - t0)}ms]`;

  try {
    const duckdb = await import('https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@duckdb/duckdb-wasm@1.32.0/+esm');
    out.textContent += `\n${ms()} DuckDB-WASM module imported`;

    const JSDELIVR_BUNDLES = duckdb.getJsDelivrBundles();
    const bundle = await duckdb.selectBundle(JSDELIVR_BUNDLES);
    out.textContent += `\n${ms()} Bundle selected: ${bundle.mainModule}`;

    const worker_url = URL.createObjectURL(
      new Blob([`importScripts("${bundle.mainWorker}");`], { type: 'text/javascript' })
    );
    const worker = new Worker(worker_url);
    const logger = new duckdb.ConsoleLogger();
    const db = new duckdb.AsyncDuckDB(logger, worker);
    await db.instantiate(bundle.mainModule, bundle.pthreadWorker);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(worker_url);
    out.textContent += `\n${ms()} DuckDB instantiated`;

    const conn = await db.connect();

    // Register same-origin parquet into DuckDB-WASM's virtual FS
    const parquet_url = new URL('./test.parquet', window.location.href).href;
    await db.registerFileURL(
      'test.parquet',
      parquet_url,
      duckdb.DuckDBDataProtocol.HTTP,
      false
    );
    out.textContent += `\n${ms()} Registered test.parquet`;

    const result = await conn.query(
      "SELECT * FROM 'test.parquet' WHERE project = 'llm' LIMIT 5"
    );
    const rows = result.toArray().map(r =>
      Object.fromEntries(
        Object.entries(r).map(([k, v]) => [k, typeof v === 'bigint' ? v.toString() : v])
      )
    );
    out.textContent += `\n${ms()} Query returned ${rows.length} rows`;
    out.textContent += `\n\nSAMPLE ROWS:\n${JSON.stringify(rows, null, 2)}`;

    const count_result = await conn.query(
      "SELECT project, count(*) AS n FROM 'test.parquet' GROUP BY project ORDER BY n DESC"
    );
    const counts = count_result.toArray().map(r =>
      Object.fromEntries(
        Object.entries(r).map(([k, v]) => [k, typeof v === 'bigint' ? v.toString() : v])
      )
    );
    out.textContent += `\n\nCOUNTS BY PROJECT:\n${JSON.stringify(counts, null, 2)}`;

    await conn.close();
    out.textContent += `\n\n✅ DuckDB-WASM PASSED in ${Math.round(performance.now() - t0)}ms`;
    out.textContent += `\n   WebR/Shinylive loading in Shinylive iframe below (separate browsing context) — check DevTools Memory…`;
    out.style.color = '#0f0';

  } catch (e) {
    out.textContent += `\n\n❌ DuckDB-WASM ERROR: ${e.message}\n${e.stack || ''}`;
    out.style.color = '#f55';
  }
</script>

## Shinylive / WebR block (native chunk — Shinylive iframe, parent page runs DuckDB-WASM)

The Shiny app below is bundled at render time via the `shinylive` Quarto filter and
rendered by the Shinylive runtime inside a managed `<iframe>`. WebR runs inside that
iframe's browsing context — separate from the parent page where DuckDB-WASM runs. If the
DuckDB-WASM block above shows ✅ AND this app reports "WebR alive" with an R version, then
both runtimes coexist in the same browser tab without OOM or Worker conflicts. That answers
the tab-coexistence question from epic #83. Full same-context integration (sharing data
without postMessage) would require a different architecture (e.g., plumber2 + DuckDB-WASM
with the Shiny app making fetch() calls to a local plumber endpoint).

```{shinylive-r}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 280
library(shiny)
ui <- fluidPage(
  h3("WebR alive — DuckDB-WASM should also be ✅ in the panel above"),
  p(paste("WebR initialised at", Sys.time())),
  verbatimTextOutput("r_info")
)
server <- function(input, output) {
  output$r_info <- renderPrint({
    list(
      r_version    = R.version.string,
      session_time = Sys.time(),
      message      = "WebR is running in a Shinylive iframe (separate browsing context from DuckDB-WASM parent page)."
    )
  })
}
shinyApp(ui, server)
```

## Verdict recording

After observing both panels, record in README.md:

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| DuckDB-WASM output shows ✅ | |
| Shinylive iframe shows "WebR alive" | |
| Combined memory < 2 GB | |
| No Worker conflicts in Console | |
| Total cold-load time (both runtimes) | |
